rulings.Case Title: KENTON SHAIFFER et al VS. DURAMETALLIC CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Cleaver-Brooks, Inc'S
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, February 19, 2014 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Cleaver-Brooks, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Shaiffer was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar because it did not demonstrate that plaintiffs failed to provide meaningful responses to comprehensive interrogatories designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiffs have to support their contention of liability. Defendant merely demonstrated that plaintiffs submitted solely objections in response to defendant¿s special interrogatories. However, even if defendant had sustained its initial burden, Mr. Shaiffer¿s deposition testimony and declaration and the Cleaver-Brooks drawings (plaintiffs¿ Exhibit D) would create a triable issue whether Mr. Shaiffer was exposed to an asbestos-containing product or material (gasket) attributable to defendant. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
